market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: energy (+27.3% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+112.3% YTD) | 4 secteur(s) en refroidissement (3M ≤ -2.0%) | 52W leader: information-technology (+57.7% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: energy (+27.5% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: financials, healthcare | Régions en difficulté: india
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: information-technology(favored,b=1.5,d=1.3%), financials(avoided,b=0.91,d=-0.23%), materials(favored,b=0.81,d=0.35%), industrials(favored,b=0.96,d=-0.27%), consumer-discretionary(neutral,b=1.22,d=0.44%), utilities(neutral,b=0.37,d=-0.62%), communication-services(neutral,b=1.19,d=1.59%), healthcare(neutral,b=0.53,d=1.47%), energy(favored,b=-0.12,d=-0.11%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.44,d=-0.5%), consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.24,d=-0.2%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Mixed signals: Brent elevated at $90 but off 5d highs, CPI 2.4% headline OK but PCE 3.77% and core MoM 0.38% signal sticky inflation, Fed on hold, VIX 22 mildly elevated but not stressed, curve positively sloped at +48bps, HY spreads tight at 271bps suggesting no credit stress. No clear directional regime — not quite stagflation (growth not collapsing, spreads tight) but inflation persistence prevents goldilocks.

adjustments.action: Increase energy cap on elevated Brent and sector leadership
adjustments.rationale: Brent $90+ with energy leading YTD (+27.3%) and 6M (+27.5%), negative beta (-0.12) provides portfolio diversification in uncertain regime.

adjustments.action: Maintain AI/semi exposure but trim slightly on valuation concern
adjustments.rationale: IT favored by RADAR (+57.7% 52W) but sticky inflation + 10Y at 4.48% creates duration headwind for high-multiple growth; trim modestly, don't abandon.

adjustments.action: Maintain gold allocation despite 14% drawdown from ATH
adjustments.rationale: Gold at $4581 is 14% off ATH — the drawdown is a position-sizing opportunity not a sell signal; sticky PCE 3.77% + geopolitical oil premium supports gold as inflation/tail hedge.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS and short-duration treasuries, avoid extending duration
adjustments.rationale: PCE 3.77% well above target with core MoM 0.38% (annualizing ~4.5%) makes TIPS attractive; 5Y breakeven at 2.54% underprices realized inflation, short duration protects if Fed forced to hike.

adjustments.action: Avoid high yield — spreads too tight for risk taken
adjustments.rationale: HY OAS at 271bps is historically tight — asymmetric risk: minimal carry pickup vs significant spread widening potential if growth slows or oil shock materializes.

adjustments.action: Add modest healthcare exposure as defensive ballast
adjustments.rationale: Healthcare daily +1.47% showing relative strength despite RADAR flagging difficulty; low beta (0.53) provides defensive quality in uncertain regime with VIX 22.

adjustments.action: Increase dividend/value tilt for Stable profile
adjustments.rationale: In neutral regime with sticky inflation and 4.48% 10Y, dividend value provides income + lower duration equity exposure vs growth; materials and industrials favored by RADAR support value tilt.

adjustments.action: Modest cash buffer given VIX 22 and mixed signals
adjustments.rationale: VIX 22 with mixed macro signals warrants small dry powder reserve; Stable already holds CLTL/GBIL/VGSH as cash proxies yielding ~4% so true cash kept minimal.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Sticky inflation (PCE 3.77%, core MoM 0.38%) with Fed on hold demands TIPS overweight and short duration; HY spreads too tight to justify risk, IG acceptable but keep duration under 4-5y.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX 22 + regime uncertainty warrants modest dry powder; Stable's existing T-bill holdings (CLTL 9%, GBIL, VGSH) already serve as productive cash so true cash kept at 2%.

ai_response._cash_guidance: MINIMUM CASH: Agressif 2% if VIX>20 (option d'achat sur la vol), Modéré 3%, Stable 0% (T-bills = cash productif). Le cash est une POSITION, pas une absence de position.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Neutral regime with VIX 22 and sticky inflation — crypto is a risk asset that underperforms in tightening scares; cap reduced from max but not eliminated given no active crisis.

warnings: PCE 3.77% vs CPI 2.4% divergence is extreme — PCE is Fed's preferred measure, market may be underpricing hawkish pivot risk; core MoM 0.38% annualizes to ~4.6%
Brent $90 with 5d avg $94 suggests oil supply disruption or geopolitical premium — further spike to $100+ would tip regime toward stagflation
HY spreads at 271bps are near cycle tights — any growth scare could cause violent repricing; asymmetric downside
Gold 14% off ATH — if this is forced liquidation (margin calls) rather than fundamental, could signal hidden stress elsewhere
2s10s at +48bps after likely inversion period — curve re-steepening historically precedes recession by 6-12 months
Dollar at DTWEXBGS 119.3 (neutral-to-weak) with EUR/USD 1.168 — weak dollar supports EM and commodities but signals potential loss of confidence if it accelerates
